_instructions.how_to_add: Copy a project block below and fill in the details for a new project

projects.title: hEXAPOD
projects.desc: A light that turns on automatically when it gets dark!
projects.fullDesc: Build a light-sensitive circuit using an LDR (Light Dependent Resistor). When the room gets dark, the Arduino detects the low light level and automatically turns on the LED or a small lamp. This is exactly how street lights and automatic night lamps work!
projects._image_note: ADD: Photo or diagram of the Smart Light project

projects.title: Weather Station
projects.desc: Read temperature & humidity — display it on screen!
projects.fullDesc: Build a real weather monitoring station using a DHT11 sensor. It reads temperature and humidity from the environment and displays it on a small LCD screen. You can even add it to your room and track how the weather changes throughout the day!
projects._image_note: ADD: Photo of Weather Station project with LCD display

projects.title: Mini Robot
projects.desc: A robot that moves, avoids walls, follows commands.
projects.fullDesc: Build a two-wheeled robot from scratch! It uses ultrasonic sensors to detect obstacles and automatically changes direction to avoid them. You can also control it manually using Bluetooth from your phone. This is the foundation of all autonomous vehicles!
projects._image_note: ADD: Photo of completed Mini Robot project

projects.title: IoT Dashboard
projects.desc: Control your device from your phone, anywhere in the world!
projects.fullDesc: Connect your Arduino to the internet using an ESP8266 WiFi module and control LEDs and sensors from a web dashboard on your phone. This project introduces you to the Internet of Things — the technology behind smart homes, smart cities, and Industry 4.0!
projects._image_note: ADD: Photo of IoT Dashboard project with phone control

projects.title: 3D Printed Part
projects.desc: Design a custom part & physically print it using 3D printer.
projects.fullDesc: Design your own custom enclosure, bracket, or gadget using Tinkercad's 3D design tools. Then physically print it on the lab's 3D printer. You'll learn the basics of 3D modelling, understand how 3D printers work layer-by-layer, and get a real physical object you designed yourself!
projects._image_note: ADD: Photo of 3D printed part / student's design

projects.title: Smart Alert System
projects.desc: Motion sensor that sends a notification when triggered.
projects.fullDesc: Build a home security system using a PIR (Passive Infrared) motion sensor. When motion is detected, it triggers a buzzer alarm AND sends a notification to your phone via WiFi. This is the same technology used in real home security systems and automatic lights in offices!
projects._image_note: ADD: Photo of Smart Alert System project
